title: Project Metadata, Python Packaging, Entry Points, Plugins, and Clean Installs · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs project metadata, python packaging, entry points, plugins, and clean installs from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for installed reproducible static-site command after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Declare build backend, project metadata, Python bounds, dependencies, optional groups, entry point, package data, license, and version source in pyproject.toml.
Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.
Load packaged templates, schemas, and default assets through supported resource APIs rather than current working directory assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review publishing team handles case 6413 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A successful editable install proves the release artifact works.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries. Artifact under review: installed reproducible static-site command.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Clean Install, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Clean Install record says installed reproducible static-site command looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Clean Install.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Clean Install for installed reproducible static-site command.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.
